query: R packages for visualizing spiral patterns

retrieved_docs.title: Nuclear Medicine Computed Tomography Physics -- Function -- Spiral scanners
retrieved_docs.content: The object continuously moves through the gantry in the Z-direction in spiral CTs. In contrast, the x-ray tube and detector assembly move around the object in a circular rotatory motion in the XY plane to acquire the images continuously (see Image . Spiral CT Machines). A single data set represents the volume covered in a particular number of spiral turns. The spiral CTs are also called helical CTs due to varying names used by manufacturers, not actual differences in modality. The spiral CTs allowed the entire organ to be imaged in a single breath-hold.

retrieved_docs.title: Coronary CT Angiography -- Technique or Treatment -- Post-Processing
retrieved_docs.content: For patients with irregular R-R intervals, like those with atrial fibrillation, selecting a specific phase of the R-R interval can lead to the reconstruction of different cardiac cycle phases and artifacts. To address this, the ECG is edited by selecting the phase using a specific time duration from the preceding R wave, such as 200 ms. The resulting artifact from beat-to-beat variability is known as a misalignment or banding artifact.

retrieved_docs.title: Humphrey Visual Field -- Normal and Critical Findings -- Interpretation of Automated Perimetry
retrieved_docs.content: Since the lower 5% of the data collected from the normal population is considered abnormal, all the abnormal data on the printout does not imply a disease state. The numerical values in the total deviation numerical plot are statistically analyzed and represented as a total deviation probability plot. The probability plot is color-coded, with lighter to darker shades representing P -values from less than 5% to those less than 0.5%. The probability plot demonstrates the extent or pattern of the field defect.

retrieved_docs.title: CT-scan Image Production Procedures -- Issues of Concern -- Software Conversion of the Data Into Images
retrieved_docs.content: The digital signal is stored as bits and bytes on the processor chip as a permanent, fixed, "original" set of data that contains information about the scanned 3D structure. This data can be manipulated using mathematical algorithms to represent the 3D structure in different ways as different types of images. The mathematics of these manipulations is complex and will be described only briefly in superficial terms. In general, formulas are applied to convert the data from its original reference values (those determined by the process described in the prior section) to new values using a different reference, a process that can be referred to as a "transformation." When the original byte values representing photon intensity (and scanned object density) are multiplied by a linear coefficient, this process is termed "linear transformation."

retrieved_docs.title: Amsler Grid -- Technique or Treatment
retrieved_docs.content: Other techniques/instruments which may be used to document scotoma include- Perimeter- Static (Humphrey field analyzer, the 10-2 protocol for central scotoma) or dynamic (Goldmann perimeter, tangent screen), or both static and kinetic (Octopus) Microperimetry- This machine checks the visual threshold of a specific area of the retina so that a correlation between structure and function is possible in a pictorial manner on an overlay of the fundus image. It also identifies any preferred retinal loci. The available machines are Nidek MP-3 (Nidek Co. Ltd., Aichi, Japan), and Macular Integrity Assessment microperimeter (MAIA, CentreVue, Padova, Italy). [17]

retrieved_docs.title: Nuclear Medicine Computed Tomography Physics -- Function -- Spiral scanners
retrieved_docs.content: The slip ring technology led to the development of spiral CTs, which became available in 1990; however, its clinical acceptance took another 2 years. [21] In a conventional CT scanner, the collimated x-ray beam is rotated in a circular motion around the object or the patient to measure the attenuation profiles from many angular positions and generate various image planes. The images are reconstructed, and the object is pushed for a small increment within the gantry for the subsequent measurement. This procedure is repeated, and the high-quality images are generated slice by slice. This imaging method, called axial imaging or the "step-and-shoot method, " takes longer. The patient remains still during the scan to avoid blurry images or motion artifacts. A shorter scan duration remained a primary requirement to avoid motion-related artifacts, leading to the invention of spiral CT scanners.

retrieved_docs.title: Exploratory Data Analysis: Frequencies, Descriptive Statistics, Histograms, and Boxplots -- Issues of Concern -- Histograms
retrieved_docs.content: Image . Figure 3 provides examples of histograms. In Figure 3A, 2 possible outliers causing kurtosis are observed. If values within 3 standard deviations are used, the result in Figure 3B are observed. This histogram appears much closer to an approximately normal distribution with the kurtosis being treated. Remember, all evidence should be considered before eliminating outliers. When reporting outliers in scientific paper outputs, account for the number of outliers excluded and justify why they were excluded.

retrieved_docs.title: Exploratory Data Analysis: Frequencies, Descriptive Statistics, Histograms, and Boxplots -- Issues of Concern -- Histograms
retrieved_docs.content: Additionally, histograms reveal outliers: data points either entered incorrectly or truly very different from the rest of the sample. When there are outliers, one must determine accuracy based on random chance or the error in the experiment and provide strong justification if the decision is to exclude them. [3] Outliers require attention to ensure the data analysis accurately reflects the majority of the data and is not influenced by extreme values; cleaning these outliers can result in better quality decision-making in clinical practice. [4] A common approach to determining if a variable is approximately normally distributed is converting values to z scores and determining if any scores are less than -3 or greater than 3. For a normal distribution, about 99% of scores should lie within three standard deviations of the mean. [5] Importantly, one should not automatically throw out any values outside of this range but consider it in corroboration with the other factors aforementioned. Outliers are relatively common, so when these are prevalent, one must assess the risks and benefits of exclusion. [6]

retrieved_docs.title: Pattern Electroretinogram -- Technique or Treatment -- Data Display System
retrieved_docs.content: Traditionally the stimulus for PERG has been displayed on a cathode-ray tube (CRT) monitor. Liquid crystal display (LCD) and light-emitting diode (LED) displays can have a flash artifact when the pattern reverses, which complicates the recorded response and no longer generates a PERG but rather a hybrid flash electroretinogram coupled with a PERG. This is hard to interpret as the waveform components no longer correspond to the anatomical areas listed above and thus should be avoided. It should be ensured that if LCD or LED displays are used, it is devoid of a flash artifact.

retrieved_docs.content: is a common extension of the basic abdominal protocol. Additional sub‑types—“Computerized tomography of chest,” “Computerized tomography of chest, abdomen and pelvis,” and “Computerized tomography of brain, chest and abdomen”—are defined as children of the generic CT concept, reinforcing their status as specialized branches of the overall modality. Routine chest x‑ray serves as a foundational reference for many of these advanced studies, being the primary mapping source for chest‑focused and multi‑region CT examinations, including those that incorporate the brain. This structured network clarifies how basic radiographic exams (routine chest x‑ray and pelvic/abdominal CT) act as reference points for a wide array of specialized, often contrast‑enhanced, CT protocols across the chest, abdomen, pelvis and brain, facilitating clinicians’ understanding of protocol relationships, diagnostic pathways, and staging frameworks.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 19 -- Level 4 -- Community 2848
retrieved_docs.content: Computerized tomography of brain and chest and Computerized tomography of brain and chest with intravenous contrast medium are both procedures that are primarily mapped from the computerized axial tomography (CT) scan head, indicating that the head CT protocol serves as the foundational reference for these combined brain and chest imaging studies. retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 9 -- Level 2 -- Community 516
retrieved_docs.content: CT angiography of thorax with contrast, Computed tomography, thorax, diagnostic; without contrast material, followed by contrast material(s) and further sections, Computerized axial tomography (CT) scan, التصوير المقطعي المحوسب للصدر, Other chest x‑ray and the category “ct scan chest” are interrelated components of thoracic imaging. The “ct scan chest” category represents chest computed tomography and includes specific techniques such as CT angiography of the thorax with contrast, which is used to visualize thoracic vascular structures, and the diagnostic thoracic CT protocol that begins without contrast and proceeds with contrast-enhanced sections. Computerized axial tomography (CT) scan is the broader imaging modality that has the child “التصوير المقطعي المحوسب للصدر” (chest CT), positioning chest CT as a specialized subset of general CT scanning. In turn, chest CT serves as a parent category for both the specialized CT angiography and the diagnostic thoracic CT protocol, as well as for Other chest x‑ray, indicating that Other chest x‑ray is considered a related chest imaging study within the same hierarchical framework. This organized hierarchy clarifies how general CT technology narrows down to chest‑specific applications and how various chest imaging modalities are grouped for clinical decision‑making and patient care.
